reviews.review_description: I cant believe i bought this. Do not make the same mistake. Ive never seen an app this bad yet. Its about the price of a drink so i will live but oh man i was taken.

reviews.review_title: Instant no refound on this application..
reviews.review_description: Carefull with purching this application
No matter what you press a new browser window opens and way too close.  You can't close them or browse.
Does not support Netflix or Hulu.

A nice and helpfull tool. 
But all the handling is very basic. 
So you have to grab the middle of your virtual screens directly with your controller. That makes it impossible to place them in an area wich is not reacheable by hand (ceiling or high walls or areas behind your walls).

And the possibility to save different configured rooms is missing.

Also the browser functionality is very basic and limited.

I would love to see updates. Is there still support?
$5 for a browser that doesn't even support WebXR?

✅I like how I am able to freely resize and place browser windows.  I am nearsighted, and love being able to plant a mega sized E*Trade window on a wall.

❌It would be "better" if it also supported a "dark mode."  For example, the fonts on the help screens would be more readable, if a dark mode was supported.

❌It would also be nice if the virtual keyboard supported hand tracking.

reviews.review_title: Read comments, what I want in developmen
reviews.review_description:
Happy with what I see so far. I agree with what others are recommending. As an immersed power user, these features should be at the forefront.

1. Keyboard / mouse support
2. Able to move monitors without physically moving them.
3. Having different backgrounds except passthrough
4. Tied for 3rd, tabs

Overall you may save me from having to buy a quest 3 (using pro). Thank you!
Don't buy
This app even don’t start on Quest 3.

developer_response.body:
Hi ku6ep,

Reality Browser has been throughly tested with Quest 3. The performance boost of the Quest 3 and color passthrough make the app even better than on Quest 1 and 2.

Please drop us an email at contact@reality-browser.com with some more detail and we'll be happy to get you up and running.

reviews.review_title: Local non-standard port sites not workin
reviews.review_description:
I love the idea of this and tried it out as soon as I found it, but much of the stuff I want to surf is local sites using non-standard ports, none of which seems to work.  Can this be fixed?
Example: http[s]://192.168.1.111:88

Is it possible for you to allow each window to play audio and video simultaneously? 

^^ That's the key thing I need from this app.

I would also like the ability to push windows away with the joystick for example, on to the ceiling. 
Rather than having to place them where I want. 

This is brilliant so far and I'm very excited by the possibilities that this app creates.
Well done and thank you.
Terrible experience, anti-human design, very poor picture adjustment, and it has not been updated for a year.

This browser is fast and well made, but could you PLEASE add support for Netflix. It says error when I try to play a Netflix video. I’ve been trying to find a way to stream Netflix from my standalone quest 2 other than the official Netflix app that only streams in sd. PLEASE do this soon! Please read!

reviews.review_title: Great app but lack clearer tutorial
reviews.review_description: Limitless stacking of windows, can move wherever you want (not stuck to 1 row like the default browser). But for the life of me, I can't figure out how to close the windows I've opened. Also it's unfortunate that you can't play multiple YouTube videos at once. Hopefully... There's updates coming...

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review! Whilst you are holding a window (with the grab button) press either 'B' or 'Y' depending on which hand you are holding the window with. This will remove it. You're right, this requires a better explanation in the tutorial.
We have been doing a huge re-write of the app from the ground. Be ready for something awesome.

reviews.review_description: As it is easy for us to change the direction of the window, it will be useful if we can change the aspect ratio, or switch between horizon view and vertial view. And the surrouding view is also an essential founction.

I move about a lot and I research lots of stuff but I find I still tend to use my mobile …until now! Now with this app I can move around my home safely and browse in vr thank u so much I feel that we need these things and amazed that this was not a feature at the start of standard alone headsets so thank you the developer for doing this as it should be ! 

I believe u will have a good future in vr and technology nice work!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much spoonbaby. We are always thrilled to hear how Reality Browser is helping our users and boosting their productivity. Our vision is that people will use AR/VR browsing in favour of mobile/laptop/desktop browsers and hearing that we have achieved that for you makes us VERY happy!

reviews.review_description: absolutely fantastic. love it to bits. my default browser now. but may i suggest changes the grab screen button from left trigger to one of the buttons on same controller. the left trigger is a button that is easily touched by mistake . sometimes ill walk to the edge of my boundry and enlarge a screen to room size for watching videos. if i then press the grab trigger by mistake it brings the screen towards me again and i have to repeat the process. i suppose this could also be done by changing the grab options. when you aim left pointer at screen you can enlarge or decrease size of screen . if the grab screen button option was changed from pulling the screen towards you immediate to a combination of pressing grab trigger then using left stick up or down to bring screen further or closer to yourself . this would be an amazing change to to an already fantastic browser. definately deserves 5 stars already

developer_response.body:
Hi shaneoculus71,
Thanks for the feedback regarding the distance grab interaction this is something that has been suggested by a number of users so we are looking to implement it ASAP!

reviews.review_description:
Your APP is amazing ! Congrats ! To me, i'm sure you are the future of display
What is great :
- before installing this, i was living in the past ;)
- fluid, smooth, works very well even with lots of windows
- memorise all your setup and the opened tabs once you launch the app again
- presence of essential options : bookmarks, private browser 

Suggestions, your APP could be even better with this, and i hope you will update) :

- If you add a very large image and you are feets away, the dot pointer is too small, i often barely see it. You should add a more visible pointer or adapt size for very large or far screens
- could be great to let people access to an adblocker into your browser because ads are pain. Netflix displays an error.
- you should, just like ALTSPACE editor, allow user to "throw" the window by holding joystick up. I put image on my roof, but todo that, i had to climb on my bed. should be nicer to be able to drag the window far away with stick, just like your option of getting the image bigger or smaller, but for the ditance
- i suggest you to add a "lock" option, in order to avoid mistake by grabbing a window accidentally, and be force to climb on your bed again HAHA
- It could be nice to be able to change the window format by manipulating an angle option, for example to add a window in portrait for a wall poster !
- in the same principle, could be amazing if the webpage itself could rotate inside a portrait windows, a bit like the gyroscopic function on a smartphone that change the webpage orientation
- just like in XSoverlay, maybe you can add an opacity function to make even more futuristic windows ! and create a window not even related to a standalone browser, but also the function to get your full PC screen(s). 
- you should add a "several setup saving systems". Could be nice to bind entire displays and and change it in a panel menu.
i had other things to say but the review page does not allow more text.
You are great, bravo bravo bravo

developer_response.body:
Great review and thanks for all the words of encouragement and detailed feature suggestions! I've put them all on the backlog. 
I'd love for Reality Browser to become THE cross platform MR browser. In order to do this I need to demonstrate demand. I'd kindly request all my wonderful users to shout about Reality Browser. Right now I'm struggling to get any mainstream reviews from our favourite VR/AR news sites and influencers. If anyone has the time please ping any inflencers or news sites.

reviews.review_description: This is already my favorite VR app, hands down, no contest. I mean, I love VR animations and a good game of chess in a virtual Escher, etc., but this? It's exactly what I needed, and I believe what many of us have been imagining and desperately hoping for in VR. To be able to easily place browser windows all around the house, resize them, even walk straight through them to get to the next... absolutely exceptional development. At one point I had 14 windows open, in 4 different rooms and lining the hallways. Ok, yeah I crashed a couple windows a couple of times, but it's a beta and I was really stomping on it hard. Because it was awesome! I was in hog heaven, a place I'd started to think was maybe just mythical. I was so impressed I really couldn't have cared less that you haven't had the chance to add more expanded features. Frankly, I can do without most of them anyway. Browsers have grown out of control, the cruft just drags things down. If I had to pin a blue ribbon on a browser for 'most robust' it wouldn't  be one with a warehouse worth of barely functional add-ons and tacky Hollywood themes de juer. It'd be the one that lets me put my FB and Insta feeds in the bathroom, my recipes in the kitchen, my maps and weather windows in the hallway by the front door, my furnace repair resources on the wall right beside the furnace, and my enormous streaming media window where the tv used to be before I found this most excellent browser.

developer_response.body:
Hey knotty_robot,
Thanks so much for the positive feedback it really means a lot! It's been a full year since we first decided that existing browsers fell short of our vision of what AR/VR should offer and we've been working hard ever since to make it a reality (no pun intended). We're so glad you like the current version. We have been focusing on the core functionality this last month with the goal of being the best VR/AR browser out there. Expect an update in the next couple of weeks!
